steps.title: Recognize what 'illustrates' the claim
steps.content: To illustrate that sophists' practices and views are foolish, we need a quotation showing a sophist directly engaged in or explaining foolish practice—not someone else's commentary about them.

steps.title: Apply this to the answer choices
steps.content: Socrates (a sophist) explains studying astronomy from a suspended basket, claiming this elevation allows his intellect to “mix with its kindred air”—his own words directly expose the foolishness of his thinking. A and C quote Strepsiades, a student of sophists rather than a sophist, while B quotes a sophist but without his flawed reasoning so plainly evident. That matches choice D — D is correct.
